title: Emotion Wheel Character
bio: Moodie Turnabout is a round little emotion wheel who spins whenever feelings get tangled. They feel curious, wear mismatched eyebrows, and keep six tiny moods tucked like pie slices.

steps.title: Base Shapes
steps.instruction: On the Guides layer, draw one large circle. Divide it into six simple pie slices with light lines from the center.
steps.hint: Do not worry if the slices are uneven; cozy wheels can wobble.

steps.title: Face Placement
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, add a tiny face in each slice using simple eyes, eyebrows, and mouths for happy, sad, mad, sleepy, scared, and silly.
steps.hint: Eyebrows are the quickest way to show each emotion.

steps.title: Personality Details
steps.instruction: On the Sketch layer, add a small center button, labels, cheek dots, tiny stars, and a little arrow pointing to one mood.
steps.hint: Keep each slice detail tiny so all six faces fit comfortably.

steps.title: Clean Lineart
steps.instruction: On the Lineart layer, trace the outer circle, slice lines, faces, center button, labels, stars, cheeks, and arrow.
steps.hint: Use clean lines on the expressions so every mood is easy to compare.

steps.title: Color & Details
steps.instruction: On the Color + Details layer, fill the wheel sections, cheeks, button, and stars. Add a soft shadow below the round character.
steps.hint: Use gentle color changes between slices without making the faces hard to see.
